dialogueChunks.intro: Report just came in, {player_name}. The situation has changed drastically.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The target doubled back. While you were tracking {location_hint}, it circled around and hit {location} again. Took more {quest_item} and injured {target_npc} in the process. This thing is smarter than we thought: it's not running, it's playing with us. We found a second trail heading in a completely different direction, and {target_npc} swears they saw at least two more {enemy_type} with it this time.
dialogueChunks.outro: It's building a force. We need to find its base before this becomes a full-scale siege.

playerResponses.text: Smart prey makes for a better hunt. I'll find its den.
You said this was one target. Now it's a warband? I need more support.
This is out of control. You need an army, not one person.

dialogueChunks.intro: We've been played, {player_name}. That trail was a decoy.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The tracks you followed? Planted deliberately to lead us in circles while the real target moved {location_hint}. A farmer near the edge of {location} saw {enemy_type} dragging something heavy through the fields at midnight. Whatever they took, they're moving it toward the old ruins. {target_npc} thinks they're fortifying a position there, which means they're not planning to leave.
dialogueChunks.outro: We've lost time, but not the hunt. Get to those ruins before they dig in any deeper.

playerResponses.text: Decoys mean they're afraid of being found. Good. I'm close.
I wasted an entire day on a false trail. This better be the right one.
I'm chasing ghosts. Someone else can take over.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, listen carefully. This just became a rescue mission.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} we've been tracking took a prisoner: {target_npc} was captured during their last raid on {location}. A runner spotted them being dragged toward a camp {location_hint}. If we go in with force, the captors might harm {target_npc} before we can reach them. But if we wait too long, they'll disappear with their hostage into the deep wilderness.
dialogueChunks.outro: We need speed and precision. Find the camp, locate {target_npc}, and bring them back alive. The {enemy_type} are secondary: the hostage is what matters.

playerResponses.text: I'll get them out. No one gets left behind.
Rescue and pursuit at once? This is going to cost extra.
A hostage situation is beyond what I signed up for.
